description: Single source of truth for the known-outcome testdata tree. Each discovered test_*.mojo file maps to the verdict the real mtest binary produces for it and the exit-code class that verdict contributes (0 = the pass/skip class, 1 = the failing class, 3 = the drift/internal-error tier). Schema /2 adds per-test expectations and the report parse `disposition` each file drives, now that the session PARSES each run's own report (the run-report-as-handshake) instead of trusting the exit status alone. Files reached only by SELECTION (testdata/matrix/, testdata/chameleon/) carry a `selection` block documenting the per-scenario selected/deselected split; DESELECTED is a summary count, never a listed row. The end-to-end harness consumes this file directly and asserts both-way completeness: every discovered test file has a row here, and every row names a file that exists on disk. There is no second, hard-coded expectations table.

exit_class_meaning.0: PASS or SKIP — contributes exit 0 (the session is clean unless another file fails)
exit_class_meaning.1: FAIL, CRASH, TIMEOUT, COMPILE-ERROR, MALFORMED-SUITE, or CAPTURE-OVERFLOW — contributes exit 1
exit_class_meaning.3: DRIFT — an off-grammar report routes to the internal-error tier (exit 3), never the run-outcome multiset

disposition_meaning.PARSED: a VALID report was parsed and its per-test rows drive the verdict
disposition_meaning.NO_REPORT: no matching report block (a crash, a timeout, or a silent binary)
disposition_meaning.AMBIGUOUS: multiple/forged report blocks — a pattern user bytes can produce
disposition_meaning.DRIFT: an off-grammar report from the pinned toolchain — the format moved
disposition_meaning.CAPTURE_OVERFLOW: output truncated with no valid report surviving in the tail

testdata/suite/test_passing.mojo.rationale: Three passing tests; the file exits 0 and emits a VALID three-row report. The exit-0 baseline and the single-file pass case.

testdata/suite/test_failing.mojo.rationale: One assertion fails; the file compiles, runs, and exits nonzero with a VALID failing report. Reported FAIL, distinct from CRASH.

testdata/suite/test_crashing.mojo.rationale: A test calls abort("simulated hard crash"); the process dies by signal 4 (SIGILL). A crash is not a failure and stays a separate outcome; the parser is unconsulted.

testdata/suite/test_zero.mojo.rationale: Collects ZERO tests and emits a VALID zero-test report, exiting 0. The verdict is now a NO-TESTS pass — PASS-from-a-parsed-zero-test-report — not the old PASS-from-exit-status ceiling. As a member of suite/ it contributes to the exit-0 class; run entirely alone it would exit 5 (nothing ran).

testdata/suite/test_noisy.mojo.rationale: Every test passes while the file floods stdout and stderr with report-shaped and timing-shaped lookalike lines; the noise precedes the real buffered report block and must not change the PASS verdict.

testdata/suite/test_compile_error.mojo.rationale: Parses cleanly (mojo format succeeds) but names an undefined symbol, so mojo build fails at name resolution. A broken test file, reported as the file's fault, never as a runner bug.

testdata/suite/nested/test_nested.mojo.rationale: A passing suite one directory down; proves the directory walk recurses.

testdata/hostile/test_silent.mojo.rationale: Compiles, runs, exits 0, prints NO report block -> ABSENT -> MALFORMED-SUITE. The file ran but spoke no report; a clean exit is no longer a PASS. The heart of the closed zero-test ceiling.

testdata/hostile/test_forger.mojo.rationale: Prints a complete VALID report, then appends a second complete block for the same path -> AMBIGUOUS -> MALFORMED-SUITE. A forged extra block never launders into a VALID verdict.

testdata/hostile/test_liar.mojo.rationale: Prints an off-grammar report (a trailing Summary with no rule before it) -> OFF_GRAMMAR -> DRIFT, which routes to exit 3. The sanctioned user-authored path to the internal-error tier; contributes nothing to the run-outcome multiset.

testdata/hostile/test_overflow.mojo.rationale: Floods stdout past the 8 MiB capture bound and exits 0 with no report surviving in the retained tail -> CAPTURE-OVERFLOW -> FAIL. A truncated capture never yields a successful verdict, never a PASS, never a drift.

testdata/slow/test_hanging.mojo.rationale: Sleeps forever. Reached ONLY under --timeout (killed and reported TIMEOUT) or the interrupt scenario. No default scenario walks slow/, so it can never hang the gate.

testdata/slow/test_slow_pass_a.mojo.rationale: A passing sibling of the hanging file that sorts after it; in the interrupt scenario it stays unscheduled and appears in the NOT-RUN accounting.

testdata/slow/test_slow_pass_b.mojo.rationale: A second passing sibling left NOT-RUN when the interrupt truncates the slow/ walk.

testdata/excluded/test_excluded.mojo.rationale: A passing suite whose role is to be the target of --exclude. When a pattern covers it the runner prints a loud EXCLUDED line and never builds it; when actually run it passes.

testdata/pkg/test_uses_pkg.mojo.rationale: Imports from a package that must be precompiled first. PASS only under --precompile testdata/pkg/mathlib, which builds the package and auto-adds its output directory to the include path so the import resolves.

testdata/matrix/test_alpha.mojo.rationale: The selection-matrix file: three distinctly named passing tests probed with --skip-all, then run under --only. A whole run passes all three; `-k two` or a node id selects one and deselects the other two; the dir+node-id union keeps all three (the plain dir operand beats the node id). DESELECTED is a summary count, never a listed row. Reached only by the selection scenarios.

testdata/matrix/test_beta.mojo.rationale: The matrix sibling: two passing tests. Its role is the mixed-operand union — `mtest testdata/matrix testdata/matrix/test_alpha.mojo::test_alpha_one` keeps the whole tree (the plain dir operand beats the node id), so both files run whole and nothing is deselected.

testdata/chameleon/test_chameleon.mojo.rationale: The chameleon: under --skip-all it lists test_real AND test_ghost (a qualifying two-test collection), but under --only it registers only test_real, so `--only test_ghost` raises the stdlib's `… test not found in suite:`. A subset selection drives the loud recollect-once, then a second identical refusal -> MALFORMED-SUITE (exit-1 class), NEVER exit 3. The sanctioned proof that user code can refuse a name it just listed.

testdata/suite/helper.mojo.rationale: No test_ prefix. A directory walk selects only test_*.mojo, so this support module must never be collected or reported; the harness proves it never appears in a default run's verdict lines.

testdata/pkg/mathlib/__init__.mojo.rationale: The importable package precompiled by the --precompile success scenario.

testdata/pkg_broken/badlib/__init__.mojo.rationale: A deliberately broken package: precompiling it fails at name resolution, driving the PRECOMPILE-ERROR banner with dependent test files listed as casualties and exit 1.
